step_by_step_reasoning: We first identify the external DNS and CDN layers with Route 53 and CloudFront, followed by an internal Route 53 that performs weighted routing. The architecture has two primary compute paths in a hybrid setup: ECS on AWS Fargate, and API Gateway with Lambda. These handle traffic cooperatively to absorb surges. Both paths connect to four backend database types: ElastiCache, DocumentDB, RDS, and OpenSearch. CloudWatch monitors ECS/Fargate performance, informing an AWS SDK-based Lambda that dynamically alters weights in the internal Route 53. The entire setup is deployed using AWS CDK.

graph.name: Zigbang Hybrid Auto-Scaling and Dynamic Weighted Architecture
graph.notes: Hybrid architecture combining ECS/Fargate for steady-state workloads and API Gateway/Lambda to handle sudden traffic spikes. Implements dynamic DNS weighted routing via an AWS SDK monitoring loop.

nodes.name: Route 53 (External)
nodes.notes: Handles initial client requests and maps to CloudFront.

nodes.name: CloudFront
nodes.notes: Global CDN caching content and routing requests to internal DNS.

nodes.name: Route 53 (Internal)
nodes.notes: Performs dynamic weight-based traffic division between ECS and Lambda.

nodes.name: ECS
nodes.notes: Orchestrates microservices for stable core traffic.

nodes.name: Fargate
nodes.notes: Serverless container compute engine running the main applications.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Ingests burst traffic during major events.

nodes.name: Lambda (Execution)
nodes.notes: Serverless backend compute protecting ECS from scaling bottlenecks.

nodes.name: AWS SDK Lambda
nodes.notes: Calculates and dynamically updates Route 53 weights based on server load.

nodes.name: CloudWatch
nodes.notes: Monitors ECS Fargate CPU and memory utilization.

nodes.name: ElastiCache
nodes.notes: Caches volatile data for rapid response times.

nodes.name: DocumentDB
nodes.notes: Stores high-read semi-structured and unstructured data.

nodes.name: RDS
nodes.notes: Houses relational structural data for core transaction processes.

nodes.name: Elasticsearch
nodes.notes: Performs address indexing/searches and centralizes application logs.

nodes.name: AWS CDK
nodes.notes: Enforces Infrastructure as Code for environment deployments.

edges.notes: Forwards traffic to weight-calculating internal load balancer layer.
Sends a dynamic percentage of stable traffic to the container cluster.
Routes burst overflow traffic to serverless endpoint.
Launches and assigns work to containers.
Submits log data and searches address indexing.
Performs address lookups and records logs.
Feeds metrics into SDK decision process.
Rewrites Route 53 weights based on active container capacity.
